p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Yellow pot, dense foliage, consistent leaf count.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Remained stable throughout the sequence; no significant growth or senescence observed.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_inference: Stable. No signs of chlorosis or turgor loss.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Black pot, central position, two primary leaves.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: Gradual decline in leaf turgor and color saturation over the 5-day period.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_inference: Stressed. Reasoning: Progressive wilting and loss of structural rigidity in the central foliage.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Black pot, two leaves, white rabbit anchor present.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: Leaf margins show increasing necrosis; the petiole angle has shifted downward.
p3_pothos.visual_health_inference: Declining. Reasoning: Necrotic tissue at leaf tips has expanded by approximately 3mm since the earliest image.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Black pot, shared with p2, small stature.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Minimal change observed; remains near the pot rim.

biome_observations.soil_surface: Soil moisture appears to be decreasing; surface texture is becoming more granular/cracked.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: No weeds or moss detected in any pots.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Debris (white particulate matter) has accumulated near the center of the pots in the most recent images.

temporal_deltas.t_minus_4_to_t_minus_3: Increased necrosis on p3 leaf edge.
temporal_deltas.t_minus_3_to_t_minus_2: Significant wilting of p2; p3 leaf angle dropped.
temporal_deltas.t_minus_2_to_t_minus_1: Stabilization of p2; p3 necrosis remains static.
temporal_deltas.t_minus_1_to_current: Appearance of white debris on soil surface; p2 shows slight recovery in posture.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is experiencing moderate environmental stress, likely due to inconsistent moisture levels or ambient humidity fluctuations. P3 is the most compromised specimen.
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of unidentified white crystalline/particulate debris on the soil surface in the current image, not present in earlier frames.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection shows a trend of slow decline, particularly in the Pothos (p3) and Mexican Mint (p2). The String of Nickels (p1) remains the most resilient. The recent appearance of debris suggests a potential external contamination or soil mineral efflorescence.
